plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and stability.
Trim SMH to 8% to reduce semiconductor concentration.
Buy NOW for a new, high-quality long setup.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall risk.
conservative.read: This conservative approach aims for a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk. Drawdowns are expected to be lower due to de-risking.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop and to reduce leverage.
Trim QQQI to 15% due to its downtrend status.
Increase QQQ to 22% for broader market exposure.
Add to SMH, bringing it to 15%, as it is an uptrending sector.
Buy PLTR for a new, strong risk/reward long setup.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month by blending broad index with quality tech. It reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for controlled growth. Market volatility can still impact returns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop, reallocating capital.
Trim QQQI to 10% to reduce exposure to the downtrend.
Increase SMH to 20% to press the strong uptrending semiconductor sector.
Add to CRWD, bringing it to 18%, to lean into uptrending AI/Software.
Buy SNOW and KLAC as new, high-scoring long setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into leverage and concentration. It carries significantly higher risk, with potential for larger drawdowns and daily decay from leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 15% to reduce growth exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 40% for a strong income focus.
Trim QQQ to 10% to further reduce growth exposure.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1%/month through distributions, prioritizing low drawdown. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in selloffs. This is not a savings account and carries market risk.
